title: chrysocephalum semipapposum subsp. asperum
intro: Chrysocephalum semipapposum subsp. asperum, also known as prickly leafed yellow buttons, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to eastern Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Chrysocephalum semipapposum subsp. asperum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
